title: Complexity Classes, Reductions, and Feasibility Boundaries · Complexity Checkpoint
summary: Explain why a requested exact optimizer needs changed scope or a bounded alternative. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a feasibility and reduction decision memo. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Translate optimization questions to decision form without losing the threshold meaning.
Describe polynomial verification and solution concepts without claiming unresolved equalities.
Map instances and answers through a polynomial reduction in the correct hardness direction.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-dsa-2 algorithm evidence portfolio."""

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Decision Problems correctness-and-cost proof for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Decision Problems assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Compare input sizes 50, 100, and 200; define the counted operation, predict the growth ratio, then challenge the claim with a reversed or duplicate-heavy input. Competency probe: Translate optimization questions to decision form without losing the threshold meaning.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Decision Problems has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
